findings.problem: The chapter runs four similar June-contact beats (call, voicemail, self-correction, shame) with limited new leverage until late, so the middle movement loops instead of escalating scene consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten or merge one of the voicemail cycles around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:88, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:96, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:184, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:194, and replace it with a concrete new road cost (money, time, safety, or missed lead) before snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:234.
findings.acceptance_test: Reading the revised sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:59, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:88, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:184, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:234, each June-contact beat must produce a distinct consequence (new information, changed decision, or immediate cost); fail if any two adjacent beats repeat only voicemail-plus-remorse without a new external stake.

findings.problem: Sanitization drift: in a high-accountability moment, Eli defaults to abstract remorse ("I know I broke enough") instead of naming the concrete harms he caused, which functions as a moral safety valve.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the voicemail at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:188, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:192, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:193 so Eli names specific actions and consequences (for example money transfer harm, deadline harm, and boundary harm) instead of generic damage language.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised voicemail anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:188 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:192, pass only if Eli names at least two concrete harms with observable consequences and avoids abstract catch-all remorse phrasing (for example "broke enough", "messed everything up").

findings.problem: Timidity: the narration introduces a hard image (handcuffs) and immediately retreats by labeling it melodramatic, reducing pressure right when the route decision should feel most coercive.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:254 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:255 to commit to either a single charged image or a fully concrete physical/economic rendering of compulsion, without self-canceling commentary.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:254 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:255, fail if the prose undercuts its own image with disclaimers (for example "melodramatic" or equivalent self-correction); pass if the emotional force is carried forward through concrete detail.

findings.problem: Nora and June converge toward the same command-and-control register in key boundary lines, flattening idiolect separation at the point where June's younger, socially adaptive voice should remain distinct.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep June's boundary firmness in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:210, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:214, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:232, but rephrase with character-specific diction/rhythm that is not interchangeable with Nora's clinical directive voice in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:162.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if June's revised lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:210, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:214, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:232 contain at least one clear age/context marker and cannot be swapped with Nora's line at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:162 without sounding wrong; fail if both characters still read as the same directive voice.

findings.problem: A repeated sentence construction ("He ended the call") appears three times in similar beat positions, creating autopilot cadence and reducing the distinct impact of each exchange.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework at least two of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:96, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:194, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:238 so each call-ending beat lands on different language and consequence rather than the same closure formula.
findings.acceptance_test: Across revised beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:96, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:194, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:238, the exact phrase "He ended the call" may appear at most once, and each call-ending beat must close on a distinct sensory or decision consequence.

findings.problem: The chapter repeats the same checklist insertion device three times (legal pad plus isolated line items), which reads as a visible prose formula instead of evolving scene texture.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep one checklist for motif continuity, but convert at least one of the list blocks anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:176 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:256 into continuous narration where numbers are embedded in action and sensory context.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter contains no more than two isolated checklist blocks across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:67, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:176, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:256, with at least one former list beat rewritten into flowing prose.

summary: Chapter 7 lands key causal beats (missed June connection, Nora deadline pressure, westbound commitment), but it does not clear pass level: the middle progression loops on similar voicemail beats, one accountability moment sanitizes harm into abstraction, and repeated prose closures/checklist formatting create mechanical texture. No canonical continuity contradiction was confirmed against the provided continuity and boundary packs, and narrative tense remains consistent with the declared past-tense standard.
